[2020-05-23 03:59] matrix30815: +vij9-XFYQz how the world did I come back with a win on this game
[2020-05-23 04:05] .bky_1556 (bky): Your opponent got doomed drones too early and they timed out; meanwhile, when your tarsiers finally arrived, they were more cost-effective than the opponent's main attackers of splitters and militia.
[2020-05-23 04:05] matrix30815: Can you explain why the tarsiers were more cost-effective
[2020-05-23 04:05] .bky_1556 (bky): Also, your first bloodrager was very good value, costing only one real damage.
[2020-05-23 04:06] .bky_1556 (bky): 4R is cheaper than 6B
[2020-05-23 04:06] matrix30815: So my strategy is getting better but I don't see it?
[2020-05-23 04:06] .bky_1556 (bky): and the delay on the first four was denying some absorb.
[2020-05-23 04:08] .bky_1556 (bky): The floating tarsiers that you'd paid for but hadn't gotten yet, and the drones-to-doomed drones difference, probably meant you felt further behind than you actually were.
[2020-05-23 04:10] matrix30815: I did felt real far behind
[2020-05-23 04:11] willflame: i'd say by turn 7 you were already ahead. you have 5 atk vs their 6 atk but you have 16 drones and they have 11, with 3 about to time out (so really closer to 8)
[2020-05-23 04:12] matrix30815: I still want to one day beat the master bot, but the random sets still hurt me when I do play it
[2020-05-23 04:12] .bky_1556 (bky): Also, the doomed drones expired just as the bot got its second round of tech. It ended up wasting multiple R or B each turn from there out, and didn't get to use its banked G for anything better than forcefields.
[2020-05-23 04:13] .bky_1556 (bky): But you were already in a decent position by the time it committed to the animus.
[2020-05-23 04:16] .bky_1556 (bky): It might help if you picked a tricky random set and tried to play it a few times in a row.
[2020-05-23 04:20] matrix30815: What do you mean pick a tricky random set and tried to play it a few times in a row. Thought it was random for a reason
[2020-05-23 04:20] .bky_1556 (bky): generate the random set,
[2020-05-23 04:20] amalloy: advice sometimes given to new players is, if the bot beats you with a strategy as P2, try playing the set again and adopt that same strategy yourself, also as P2. you'll probably lose with it, and looking at the difference between how you played it and how the bot did will help you find weaknesses in your game
[2020-05-23 04:21] .bky_1556 (bky): then play the same set again after losing a game.
[2020-05-23 04:21] amalloy: and if you win, then you've at least learned a decent way to play a strategy that is not terrible
[2020-05-23 04:25] matrix30815: What would be a wacky 8 set to try out on both sides
[2020-05-23 04:26] amalloy: any set will be interesting to study
[2020-05-23 04:27] amalloy: you could also try a set that someone else has already played, and then compare your line to theirs after you finish
[2020-05-23 04:35] .bky_1556 (bky): I stand by my recommendation. Any set you lose in is one that's good for you to try on both sides.
[2020-05-23 04:37] .bky_1556 (bky): The caveat there is that an interesting set for you won't be the same as an interesting set for a Tier X ranked player.
